decisions.title: Shared server resolver is authoritative
decisions.recommendation: Create or extract a server-side workflow phase skill resolver consumed by authoring, validation, import/export, runtime prompt assembly, summary/report state, and desktop APIs.
decisions.rationale: Settings, desktop types, runtime loader, and SkillTool currently expose different skill source vocabularies. A shared resolver prevents drift.
alternatives_considered: Desktop-only resolution through skillStore: rejected because import/export and runtime need server diagnostics.
Fully qualified references everywhere: rejected because the selected product model is names-first with metadata only when needed.
Treat unsupported runtime sources as missing: rejected because unsupported-source is more truthful and preserves portability.

decisions.title: Names-first binding with optional provenance
decisions.recommendation: Extend existing phases[].skills entries into WorkflowPhaseSkillReference while preserving old { name, reason } entries and unknown fields.
decisions.rationale: The registry and validation code already preserve unknown fields. Reusing phases[].skills avoids a parallel schema and keeps old templates valid.
alternatives_considered: New separate phaseSkillBindings array: rejected because it adds migration and duplicate semantics.
Copy skill applicability metadata into templates: rejected by FR-007 and MP-007.

decisions.title: Dependency manifest on export, no default bundle
decisions.recommendation: Add a dependency manifest with resolver snapshots to workflow export packages while keeping old exports importable and excluding skill package contents.
decisions.rationale: The existing export package has a natural additive extension point, while default bundling is explicitly deferred for security and provenance reasons.
alternatives_considered: Names-only export: rejected because recipients need dependency diagnostics.
Bundle skill files by default: rejected by MP-010 and MP-016.

decisions.title: Runtime prompt uses recommendation snapshot
decisions.recommendation: Resolve phase references into a persisted snapshot and render a distinct active-phase Recommended phase skills block from that snapshot.
decisions.rationale: Current runtime only renders Skill guidance. The new semantics require stronger active-phase attention while preserving non-mandatory SkillTool behavior.
alternatives_considered: Auto-invoke recommended skills: rejected by MP-003, FR-003, and CA-010.
Keep existing Skill guidance wording: rejected because FR-014 requires stronger active-phase emphasis.

decisions.title: Evidence is bounded and signal-bearing
decisions.recommendation: Persist evidence only for recommended skills that were used, clearly relevant but skipped, or clearly relevant but unavailable.
decisions.rationale: FR-016 and MP-009 require lightweight evidence. Exhaustive unused-skill checklists would create noise and weaken reports.
alternatives_considered: Store every recommendation as unused evidence: rejected as noisy.
Use analytics-only SkillTool usage: rejected because workflow reports and resume need durable state.

decisions.title: SkillTool remains the safety boundary
decisions.recommendation: Workflow runtime must never apply skill-derived tools, shell behavior, hooks, forked agents, model, or effort changes directly.
decisions.rationale: SkillTool validates names, checks permissions, applies context modifiers, handles forked execution, and records usage.
alternatives_considered: Pre-grant allowed tools for recommended skills: rejected as a safety-boundary violation.
Copy skill metadata into workflow runtime: rejected by FR-007 and CA-004.

decisions.title: UI uses existing workflow surfaces
decisions.recommendation: Implement authoring, import/export diagnostics, and runtime status inside WorkflowTemplateEditor, WorkflowImportExportDialog, and WorkflowStatusPanel.
decisions.rationale: These are the existing surfaces for template authoring, sharing, and runtime inspection. A new workflow skill page would duplicate ownership.
alternatives_considered: Separate workflow skill management page: rejected as duplicate inventory.
Raw textarea only: rejected because FR-017 requires a phase-local selector.

assumptions: Content hash or version markers can be derived from available skill metadata or file contents without logging skill contents.
The first implementation can expose unsupported-source diagnostics for sources that cannot safely share runtime loader parity in one slice.
Existing reason fields can be preserved as legacy advisory data without becoming authoritative applicability metadata.
risks: Skill source vocabulary differs between server API, desktop types, and runtime loader; tasks must close this with shared types and tests.
Existing skillProvenance fields currently mean template guidance; implementation must avoid mixing legacy guidance with resolved skill evidence.
Prompt emphasis must be strong enough to matter without creating hidden mandatory behavior.
